observable_oracle: A linked-worktree-style .git file always routes to Git preflight.
failure_risk: A gitfile checkout could be misclassified as terminal non-Git.

observable_oracle: A plain directory with no Git control entry in its physical ancestor chain is definitely non-Git; a sibling repository does not cross the root boundary.
failure_risk: Cold non-Git code structure could regress to avoidable Git subprocesses or sibling-root leakage.

observable_oracle: A loaded root whose final filesystem node is a symlink fails open to Git preflight.
failure_risk: Symlink retargeting could make a terminal non-Git classification stale or cross an unseen repository boundary.

observable_oracle: Two demands after a requires-preflight classification each retry a transient repository-changing result.
failure_risk: Transient Git unavailability could be terminally cached and suppress later recovery.
notes: Only definite non-Git is terminally cached.

observable_oracle: Repeated demand classifies once per root epoch, unload clears the cache, and reload reclassifies without invoking Git.
failure_risk: A terminal non-Git result could leak across root lifetimes or repeatedly probe within one epoch.

observable_oracle: A loaded subdirectory beneath an ancestor .git directory routes to Git preflight.
failure_risk: Subdirectory workspace roots could be falsely classified non-Git when the control entry exists above them.

observable_oracle: A resolved Slice 1A binding seeds a private store; equal exact duplicates consume nothing, higher request generations issue the next graph binding generation and ordinal, and later lower or equal-conflicting generations leave the current endpoint and accounting unchanged.
failure_risk: Dropping request generation or defining ambiguous generation ties could let stale work replace a current graph endpoint or double-charge accounting.

observable_oracle: Acceptance derives contributions only from resolved Slice 1A bindings and rejects root, lifetime, catalog, repository authority, schema, policy, unresolved binding, and unavailable-artifact mismatches before accounting.
failure_risk: A self-consistent forged snapshot or stale authority could mint an endpoint or consume graph budget.
notes: Renamed stale-snapshot ID to resolved Slice 1A authority fence

observable_oracle: NFC and NFD names normalize before deduplication and byte ordering; case remains distinct, every tested input permutation is stable, and swapped domains or ambiguous concatenations produce distinct framed digests.
failure_risk: Unicode-form, locale, domain, or framing ambiguity could make graph contributions non-deterministic or collide across distinct symbol sets.
notes: Slice 1B explicit Unicode and framing canonicalization

observable_oracle: Candidate permutations remain stable, and a dedicated production ordering key independently exercises the full path then file ID then binding generation then ordinal tie chain across three permutations; duplicates deduplicate and overflow returns no prefix.
failure_risk: An untested comparator tie could preserve input order and make duplicate-definition traversal permutation-dependent.

observable_oracle: The shared side-aware validator rejects every graph-key component on both source and target sides; real projectable foreign targets and independent-store targets are rejected, while current same-store endpoints construct one accounted edge.
failure_risk: Checking only the source endpoint or only part of the graph key could admit a foreign target across authority, schema, policy, or store boundaries.

observable_oracle: Store accounting reaches exact cumulative posting, byte, and two-edge boundaries; rejects the second contribution or edge at N+1 without mutation; retains single-dimension exact/N+1 checks; and types overflow in every dimension with Sendable value contracts.
failure_risk: A cumulative-only off-by-one or partial mutation on rejection could exceed graph limits even when individual additions fit.

observable_oracle: Factories and final result validation reject self or selected-peer targets; proven-missing records require exact complete catalog and repository authority and fail under unknown, partial, unavailable, stale-catalog, or foreign-authority universes; prior coverage and staleness fences remain enforced.
failure_risk: A selected source could be reintroduced as a target or an incomplete/stale universe could falsely prove a definition absent.
notes: Slice 1B selected-source exclusion and complete-only proof

observable_oracle: Direct reservations exercise the active-count bound; actor-local and cross-actor shared binding reservations exercise exact and N+1 capacity, process rejection status, and zero-count recovery after concurrent close, deinit, cancellation, invalidation, success, and retry; invalid accounting fails closed.
failure_risk: A leaked, double-released, or non-fail-closed low-priority permit could starve graph runtimes or undercount concurrent graph work.

observable_oracle: One actor holds the sole process reservation while a second actor receives the exact activeReservationCountLimit rebuild and query dispositions; releasing the first permit returns both counters to zero and lets the second actor publish.
failure_risk: Conflating process active-count saturation with reserved-binding capacity could report the wrong busy reason or fail to recover after the active permit closes.

observable_oracle: An equal-generation conflicting authority invalidates queries, exact-key replay cannot publish while conflict remains, and only a higher generation clears the conflict and restores ready publication.
failure_risk: A conflicting authority could leave queries invalid while a redundant rebuild misleadingly returns published or restores stale data.

observable_oracle: A generation-1 projection cannot serve generation 3 before resealing; a structurally changed live snapshot is superseded without replacing generation 1; an exact successor seal reuses the resident projection bytes and serves generation 3; and a same-generation invalid snapshot remains fail-closed despite successor replay.
failure_risk: A stale proof could serve a newer generation, a mismatched successor could replace the last valid shard, or successor replay could mask a same-generation authority conflict.
notes: Projection generation handoff without graph rebuild.

observable_oracle: Each node, posting, edge, and byte dimension publishes at its measured exact limit, rejects the same snapshot when its attempted total is limit plus one with the exact dimension, and leaves queries budget-unavailable.
failure_risk: An off-by-one check in any graph-size dimension could exceed work bounds or publish a truncated shard.

observable_oracle: Queries immediately distinguish not-built, rebuilding, cancelled, stale, revoked, and empty-partial missing-source states; actor admission, input-binding, and unique-target overflow paths fail closed without a truncated result.
failure_risk: A query could wait for readiness, counterfeit unavailable state as success, expose a truncated prefix, or let a graph exceed a hard bound.

observable_oracle: Fencing contributions for the current root epoch clears staged and resident projection bytes, increments revoked coverage exactly once, and makes the formerly sealed key immediately unavailable as notBuilt.
failure_risk: Path invalidation could leave stale projection coverage or retained bytes authoritative and let queries serve data from obsolete repository paths.

observable_oracle: A projection segment larger than the one-byte retained/staged limits is rejected, and the subsequent query returns a retainedProjectionBytes budget disposition whose attempted count exceeds the exact limit instead of returning targets.
failure_risk: Oversized projected data could bypass memory bounds, publish a partial target set, or surface an untyped unavailable state that callers cannot classify.

observable_oracle: When a same-generation live rebuild is blocked, staging a projection forces the late live build to finish cancelled or superseded; the exact projection seal is then accepted and the ready query returns the projected target.
failure_risk: A late live rebuild could overwrite staged projection authority, downgrade complete coverage, or prevent the exact projection from sealing.

observable_oracle: Raw selected sources accept N and reject a duplicate flood at N+1 before deduplication; two resolution edges may share one bounded unique target; reference failures accept N and reject N+1 without a prefix.
failure_risk: Dedup-before-budget or edge-count target accounting could permit unbounded input, and unbounded failure output could exhaust query work or memory.

observable_oracle: Normal and reversed ready bindings plus distinct selected-source orders yield identical canonical results; exact duplicate sources deduplicate, conflicting generations reject, selected peers disappear from both targets and resolutions, and duplicate binding or candidate overflow fails closed.
failure_risk: Input order, ambiguous selected generations, duplicate definitions, or selected peers could make graph expansion nondeterministic or unsafe.

observable_oracle: Generation-keyed publication gates independently hold N and N+1, release N+1 to publish while N remains blocked, then prove N completes superseded without replacing N+1; rejected and cancelled builds retain the previous complete shard physically while current queries fail closed.
failure_risk: A scheduler-dependent test could miss a delayed or failed rebuild overwriting current authority or exposing a partially mutated replacement.

observable_oracle: While generation 2 is staged, the generation-1 complete shard and its resident byte count remain published; a generation-2 structure query reports typed incomplete coverage without old nodes; and only the exact seal atomically publishes generation 2 as complete.
failure_risk: Replacement staging could evict the last complete shard, leak old nodes under the new key, or publish an incomplete replacement before its exact proof is sealed.

observable_oracle: A definition present solely in root B resolves there while root A remains unresolved; foreign snapshots are rejected and unload/reload lifetime identity cannot reuse the old actor or key.
failure_risk: A root filter applied after traversal could leak a root-B-only definition into root A or let an unloaded lifetime reuse stale graph authority.

observable_oracle: An unsealed staged projection may account for two resident nodes, but its structure query returns typed incomplete coverage with the exact supported-candidate and remaining counts and no structure result.

observable_oracle: Stale source generations produce no traversal, replacement shards emit only current target generations, and invalid newer input cannot partially replace the last complete shard.

observable_oracle: Budget exhaustion around a target turn returns a contiguous rendered range without holes in returned_turn_start/end.
failure_risk: A model could trust a returned range that silently omitted interior turns.

observable_oracle: A session saved after cached inventory population resolves through one cache-bypassed retry.
failure_risk: Freshly completed sessions could be invisible for the scan-cache TTL.

observable_oracle: Out-of-range stored default idle threshold is clamped to the accepted query range.
failure_risk: Corrupt settings could produce invalid duration calculations or surprising validation behavior.

observable_oracle: Calendar grouped time merges overlapping/nested turn intervals before summing active duration.
failure_risk: Calendar totals could double-count overlapping activity intervals.

observable_oracle: A successfully loaded empty live transcript contributes zero rather than stale stored duration fallback.
failure_risk: Stale duration primitives could survive after an empty transcript rewrite.

observable_oracle: A stale metadata record is detected by file signature and recomputed from the live transcript for session grouping.
failure_risk: Session/workspace totals could diverge from calendar totals after transcript growth.

failure_risk: History queries could miss workspaces, re-read stale indexes, misattribute sessions, or fail on corrupt/local session data.

observable_oracle: Copying a workspace Agent Models profile to global overwrites scalar model fields, MCP role overrides, and the full Context Builder per-agent model map in one durable write and one global notification.
failure_risk: Workspace overrides could merge stale global Context Builder model slots or notify multiple times, causing drift between global fields and scoped profiles.
notes: Work Item 1 scoped Agent Models persistence coverage.

observable_oracle: A full global Agent Models profile write decomposes into the existing scalar/global-default fields, normalizes raw values, and emits exactly one save plus one global notification.
failure_risk: Global Agent Models settings could split into a second blob or trigger duplicate saves/refreshes.
notes: Work Item 1 scoped Agent Models persistence coverage.

observable_oracle: Missing Agent Models settings resolve to an empty global profile and workspace reads do not create durable workspace profiles.
failure_risk: Read-time resolver defaults could unexpectedly mutate globalSettings.json or create inactive workspace data.
notes: Work Item 1 scoped Agent Models persistence coverage.

observable_oracle: A schema v3 document loads under v4, preserves existing global Agent Models fields, and eagerly materializes a workspace override profile when selected.
failure_risk: The v4 scoped profile field could fail to migrate prior documents or snapshot incomplete global settings.
notes: Work Item 1 scoped Agent Models persistence coverage.

observable_oracle: Switching to workspace overrides snapshots the current complete global profile in one save/notification, and copy global-to-workspace creates an active workspace profile.
failure_risk: Workspace override activation could remain partial or inactive, causing UI edits to keep inheriting global settings.
notes: Work Item 1 scoped Agent Models persistence coverage.

observable_oracle: A persisted workspace Agent Models profile survives unrelated scalar and copy-settings writes while those unrelated writes still apply.
failure_risk: Whole-document reconstruction could drop the new workspace-keyed Agent Models field during unrelated settings saves.
notes: Work Item 1 scoped Agent Models persistence coverage.

observable_oracle: Recommendation satisfaction is computed against the selected global or workspace Agent Models profile.
failure_risk: Workspace override recommendations could appear satisfied from global settings and skip needed setup.

observable_oracle: Bulk recommended setup mutates the workspace Agent Models profile and leaves the global profile unchanged.
failure_risk: Applying recommendations from a workspace override pane could overwrite global model settings.

observable_oracle: Context Builder recommendation writes the target workspace profile, preserves global profile, and does not backfill legacy chat settings.
failure_risk: Scoped Context Builder recommendations could reintroduce global/workspace drift or mutate legacy fields.

observable_oracle: MCP role-default recommendation clears only the target workspace overrides while preserving global role overrides.
failure_risk: Workspace recommendation apply could clear global role defaults used by other workspaces.

observable_oracle: A workspace role override resolves independently from a different global role override for the same role.
failure_risk: Workspace-scoped sub-agent role defaults could leak from or overwrite global defaults.

observable_oracle: WindowSettingsManager forwards scoped Agent Models writes and copy operations to the durable store without a window-local overlay.
failure_risk: Agent Models settings could appear to save in one window while remaining transient or stale elsewhere.

observable_oracle: Legacy global Context Builder setters update the global Agent Models profile and post global Agent Models change notifications for string and optional model overloads.
failure_risk: Legacy Context Builder setters could mutate global Agent Models state without notifying other windows or runtime consumers.
notes: Oracle blocker fix for scoped Agent Models refresh

observable_oracle: AgentModelsSettingsViewModel recommendation satisfaction, writes, copies, recommendation apply, and refreshes use the injected SettingsManaging profile boundary rather than the engine's separate global store.
failure_risk: The Settings window could read recommendation state or persist scoped changes through a mismatched store, showing stale satisfaction and mutating the wrong profile.

observable_oracle: Trimmed unknown Context Builder provider and model identifiers survive global and workspace round trips plus an unrelated settings write.
failure_risk: Current catalog normalization could silently discard or replace future-provider settings during load or save.

observable_oracle: Global planning, compose, sync, MCP role-default, and discovery-filter writers each notify once for a change and never for a no-op.
failure_risk: Legacy global settings surfaces could mutate the effective Agent Models profile without refreshing windows, or emit redundant refreshes for no-ops.
notes: PR #275 correction regression; five changed and five no-op writer scenarios.

observable_oracle: One external Agent Models write refreshes Prompt model state without a second save and preserves unrelated workspace copy/chat settings.
failure_risk: Broad Prompt synchronization could write unrelated settings back in response to an Agent Models notification, causing loops or collateral state changes.

observable_oracle: Unsynchronized empty Built-in Chat selection shows its placeholder, while synchronized selection displays the Oracle model.
failure_risk: The settings UI could falsely display the Oracle model as the Built-in Chat choice while synchronization is disabled.

observable_oracle: Automatic recommendation seeding remains unmarked, explicit application marks user ownership, and later automatic work cannot demote it.
failure_risk: Automatic seeding could block future initialization or overwrite a user-owned Context Builder selection because ownership intent was lost.

observable_oracle: A same-as-recommended MCP role override remains exposed as a stored override in Agent Models settings so Clear Pin and section reset controls are available.
failure_risk: Same-as-recommended pins could be persisted but hidden from Settings reset affordances, leaving no obvious way to return to recommendation tracking.
notes: PR #275 reviewer regression for stored role pins matching current recommendation.

observable_oracle: Blank Built-in Chat selections do not mirror into the Oracle model for global or workspace Agent Models profiles while sync is enabled.
failure_risk: The settings UI could reintroduce the upstream blank-chat Oracle reset through direct scoped profile writes.
notes: Oracle rebase review regression for upstream #305 scoped Agent Models path.

observable_oracle: Refreshing retained payload changes render identity only for the matching result and leaves unrelated rows stable.
failure_risk: Broad refresh invalidation could rerender unrelated transcript results or leave the changed payload stale.

observable_oracle: Catalog ingress becomes applied and explicit create reaches disk while correlated derived codemap invalidation remains gate-blocked.
failure_risk: Publisher-derived convergence could block canonical catalog visibility or explicit mutation I/O.

observable_oracle: Applied edit acknowledgement with pending freshness explicitly warns against replay and labels the operation ID as correlation-only.
failure_risk: Agents could replay an already-applied edit and duplicate or corrupt content after derived freshness lag.

observable_oracle: A freshness-pending file action remains mutation_state applied and is not represented as a blindly retryable pre-mutation failure.
failure_risk: Agents could repeat successful create, move, or delete operations after delayed derived convergence.

observable_oracle: Create selection intent persists through the canonical coordinator before a successful acknowledgement is formatted.
failure_risk: Create could report success while selection remains private or is lost before Oracle packaging.

observable_oracle: The file_actions schema describes operation IDs as response correlation only and does not promise deduplication or journaling.
failure_risk: Agents could incorrectly treat correlation IDs as replay-safe idempotency keys.

observable_oracle: Mutation preflight freshness timeout is strictly shorter than the bounded tool watchdog.
failure_risk: A blocked freshness wait could race connection teardown instead of returning a structured retryable result.

observable_oracle: Inactive unhydrated Agent mutation routes return the explicit worktree_scope_hydrating failure before path translation or mutation.
failure_risk: Mutation could fall back to the visible canonical workspace or hang without a machine-readable retry.
notes: Issue #390 initial hydration failure contract; forward-progress coverage added in this PR.

observable_oracle: Workspace freshness failure is emitted before mutation I/O and explicitly marked safe to retry.
failure_risk: A timeout after disk mutation could be misclassified as pre-write and cause destructive replay.

observable_oracle: Nested request and geo fields are removed while safe sibling telemetry remains intact.
failure_risk: Privacy-sensitive nested network or location data could escape scrubbing or safe diagnostics could be over-redacted.

observable_oracle: Request, geo, user, and stable device identifiers are removed from telemetry payloads.
failure_risk: Sentry payloads could retain stable user or device identity and precise request/location data.

observable_oracle: Wrapped mutation arguments preserve the caller operation identity through tab-context routing.
failure_risk: Routing wrappers could drop correlation identity and make mutation outcomes impossible to match to requests.

observable_oracle: Move preflight submits source and destination to one combined applied-ingress wait with one mutation timeout before structured failure mapping.
failure_risk: Two sequential freshness waits could exceed the bounded tool watchdog and lose the structured workspace freshness response.

observable_oracle: An initially unhydrated inactive persisted Agent route triggers authoritative binding hydration, re-resolves, and produces a valid physical worktree lookup with no mutation scope failure.
failure_risk: Repeated worktree_scope_hydrating responses could never initiate hydration, creating an infinite safe-retry loop.

observable_oracle: Default creation and baseline saves return and write schema v2.
failure_risk: In-memory schema could disagree with the durable header.
notes: Replaces testBaselineContentSavesAsSchemaV2 and covers default consistency.

observable_oracle: Workspace Agent Models content requires the fixed v4 feature constant.
failure_risk: A future current schema bump could relabel existing v4 content.

observable_oracle: False-v4 load makes one exact backup, changes only semantic schemaVersion, remains v1.0.28 writable, and does not repeat.
failure_risk: Automatic repair could lose unknown state or repeat backups.
notes: False-v4 success and rollback boundary regression.

observable_oracle: An otherwise eligible v4 document with no Agent Models key normalizes to v2.
failure_risk: The absent-key production shape could remain falsely stamped v4.

observable_oracle: Nonempty workspace profiles remain byte-exact v4 and create no repair backup.
failure_risk: Genuine v4 user profiles could be downgraded.

observable_oracle: Partial typed v4 content stays byte-exact and blocks automatic saves.
failure_risk: Startup seeding could overwrite an unverifiable document.

observable_oracle: Injected backup failure preserves original bytes and blocks normalization, seeding, retry, and later mutation saves.
failure_risk: A failed safety backup could be bypassed by launch-time writes.

observable_oracle: Injected atomic failure captures exact backup bytes, preserves live bytes, and blocks later saves.
failure_risk: A failed replacement could corrupt or overwrite the only live copy.

observable_oracle: Session dismissal clears on reason transition and unblock.
failure_risk: A session close action could hide a different persistence failure.
notes: Replaces durable-suppression invalidation tests with session policy.

observable_oracle: Neither session dismissal nor an obsolete stored suppression value hides a warning after relaunch.
failure_risk: Removed durable suppression could remain behaviorally active.
notes: Deletion guard replacing durable suppression/fingerprint tests.

observable_oracle: Frozen v1.0.28 writer round-trips nested copy, chat, globals, scalars through patched initialization/migration/save and rollback.
failure_risk: Patched baseline files could be unreadable or destructive in v1.0.28.

observable_oracle: MCP set returns a stable reason code and warning that state is in memory, original is preserved, and explicit recovery is required.
failure_risk: Agents could report a failed normalization as durably saved.
notes: MCP mapping for automatic schema normalization failure.

observable_oracle: Loading a valid compatible lineaged v2 document leaves every byte unchanged.
failure_risk: A read-only launch could rewrite compatible user settings.
notes: Restores the former settings.schema.load_no_rewrite contract with focused byte equality.

observable_oracle: Present null, array, and string Agent Models values preserve original bytes, reject compatible import, and block seeding, retry, and ordinary saves.
failure_risk: Invalid-present v4 content could bypass backup-first repair and be silently downstamped to v2.
notes: Final review regression for invalid-present raw Agent Models shapes.
